reasoning: Wei Yi's rating on 2025-10-16 was 2754. To gain at least 1%, his rating must increase to ~2782, which requires a +28 point jump. For top classical chess players, the K-factor is 10, meaning a player needs roughly +6 or +7 net wins over equal opponents to gain this much rating. Such massive increases are extraordinarily rare for a single month, making the probability for the November FIDE list (used on Nov 2 and Nov 25) virtually zero absent news of a historic tournament sweep. Over three months (January 2026), the window is slightly larger, but reaching 2782 would represent a massive career peak (putting him top 5 worldwide), generating a very low base rate. With no immediate evidence of him completing a dominant classical event in October, the likelihood of clearing the +1% threshold is minimal.
batch_queries: According to Wikipedia, will Wei Yi have an Elo rating on each resolution date listed below that's at least 1% higher than on 2025-10-26?
